title: 10 best herbal tinctures for chapped lips
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for chapped lips are concentrated liquid extracts made from medicinal herbs, which are known for their soothing and healing properties. These tinctures typically contain a combination of herbs such as calendula, chamomile, and St. John's wort, which have anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and analgesic effects, helping to reduce redness, irritation, and pain associated with chapped lips. By applying these herbal tinctures, individuals can experience relief from dry, cracked, and sore lips, improving their quality of life by allowing them to speak, eat, and smile without discomfort. For example, a person who suffers from severe chapped lips due to cold weather can use a calendula-based tincture to moisturize and heal their lips, enabling them to go outside and enjoy outdoor activities without worrying about their lip condition.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tincture helps with chapped lips because it contains anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties that soothe and heal dry, cracked lips. The herb's active compounds, including flavonoids and carotenoids, provide a protective barrier that locks in moisture and safeguards lips from environmental stressors, such as wind and cold temperatures. Furthermore, pot marigold tincture boasts antimicrobial qualities that help prevent infections and promote faster healing, making it an ideal natural remedy for chapped lips.
remedy_properties: Wound healing: Pot marigold tincture promotes wound healing, which is important for chapped lips as it helps to repair damaged lip tissue (active constituents
Anti-inflammatory: Its anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation in chapped lips (active constituents
Moisturizing: Pot marigold tincture has moisturizing properties that help to soothe and hydrate chapped lips, preventing further dryness and cracking (active constituents
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of pot marigold tincture help protect chapped lips from infection, promoting faster healing and preventing complications (active constituents
Analgesic: Pot marigold tincture provides pain relief, alleviating the discomfort associated with chapped lips (active constituents
remedy_recipe: Harvest pot marigold flowers at peak bloom, gently rinse, and let dry completely.
Chop or grind 2 ounces of dried flowers and place in a clean glass jar, filling three-quarters full.
Pour 8 ounces of 80-proof vodka or rum over the flowers, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for two to four weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth, discarding the plant material, and store the final product in a tightly sealed glass container.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, pot marigold flowers help soothe and heal chapped lips.
Leaves: High in vitamins and minerals, leaves from the pot marigold plant aid in moisturizing and repairing damaged lip tissue.
Seeds: Containing essential fatty acids, pot marigold seeds help to nourish and protect chapped lips from environmental stressors.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile tincture helps with chapped lips because it has anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties. The tincture, made from the flowers of the German chamomile plant, contains active compounds such as azulene and bisabolol that can soothe and reduce inflammation in dry, cracked lips. Additionally, the tincture can create a protective barrier on the lips, locking in moisture and preventing further dryness and chapping. Its natural and gentle properties make it an ideal remedy for chapped lips, providing relief and hydration without the use of harsh chemicals.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: German chamomile tincture helps reduce inflammation and irritation, providing relief for chapped lips caused by dryness or environmental factors. This property is due to the active constituent, alpha-bisabolol.

Wound-healing: The tincture promotes wound healing and tissue regeneration, aiding in the recovery of damaged lip tissue. The flavonoids and essential oils present in German chamomile contribute to this healing effect.

Moisturizing: German chamomile tincture has moisturizing properties that help keep chapped lips hydrated and prevent further dryness. The active constituent responsible for this is azulene, which also has soothing effects.

Antimicrobial: The tincture inhibits the growth of microorganisms, reducing the risk of infection in chapped lips. This property is attributed to the presence of active compounds like chamazulene and matricin.

Pain relief: German chamomile tincture has analgesic properties that help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with chapped lips. This is due to the presence of various active constituents, including bisabolol and matricin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of fresh German chamomile flowers, ideally in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Coarsely chop the flowers and place them in a clean glass jar, then pour 1 cup of 100-proof vodka over the flowers until fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
After the extraction period, strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine-mesh strainer, squeezing out any excess liquid.
Transfer the tincture to a dark glass dropper bottle and store it in a cool, dark place; apply a drop or two to chapped lips as needed.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, chamomile flowers can help soothe and heal chapped lips.
Leaves: Containing azulene and other soothing compounds, chamomile leaves can help reduce inflammation and irritation in chapped lips.
Seeds: High in essential oils, chamomile seeds can help moisturize and protect chapped lips, preventing further damage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eastern purple coneflower tincture helps with chapped lips because it contains high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties can help to soothe and heal dry, cracked lips by reducing inflammation and providing hydration. Additionally, the tincture has been shown to have antibacterial and antiviral properties, which can help to protect the lips from infection and further irritation. The herbal extract can also improve the overall health and appearance of the lips by promoting collagen production and increasing blood flow to the area. With regular use, eastern purple coneflower tincture can provide relief and nourishment to chapped lips, leaving them soft, smooth, and moisturized.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: This property helps reduce inflammation and redness in chapped lips, providing relief and promoting healing. Active constituents
Moisturizing property: Eastern purple coneflower tincture can help lock in moisture, preventing dryness and keeping lips hydrated. Active constituents
Antioxidant property: The tincture can protect lips from environmental stressors and free radicals, reducing the signs of aging and promoting healthy lip tissue. Active constituents
Antimicrobial property: This property helps prevent and treat lip infections, reducing the risk of further irritation and promoting faster healing. Active constituents
Wound-healing property: Eastern purple coneflower tincture can accelerate the healing of chapped lips by stimulating tissue regeneration and reducing recovery time. Active constituents
remedy_recipe: Harvest purple coneflower (Echinacea purpurea) flowers at peak bloom, filling a clean, dry basket; rinse flowers gently, allowing them to air dry for 24 hours.
Chop dried flowers into small pieces, filling a clean, dry mason jar halfway, then adding 80-proof vodka until flowers are completely submerged.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place, shaking it gently once a day for two to four weeks.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth into a clean, dry bottle, squeezing out excess liquid; discard the spent flowers.
Label the bottle with the tincture's contents, date, and suggested dosage (2-3 drops applied directly to chapped lips, up to three times daily).
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, the flowers help soothe and heal chapped lips.
Leaves: Containing high levels of vitamin E and essential oils, the leaves help moisturize and protect chapped lips.
Buds: High in tannins, buds have astringent properties that help relieve and heal dry, cracked lips.
Seeds: Packed with essential fatty acids and nutrients, the seeds help nourish and regenerate damaged lip tissue.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St. John's wort tincture helps with chapped lips because it contains anti-inflammatory and healing properties that can soothe and repair damaged skin. The herb's active compounds, including hypericin and hyperforin, have been shown to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation, making it an effective treatment for chapped and dry lips. Additionally, St. John's wort has moisturizing properties that can help to lock in moisture and protect the lips from further damage. Its antiviral and antibacterial effects can also help to prevent infections and promote overall lip health. By using St. John's wort tincture regularly, you can help to keep your lips moisturized, protected, and free from irritation.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: St. John's wort tincture reduces inflammation and irritation, providing relief for chapped lips caused by dryness or environmental factors. The active constituents responsible for this property are hypericin and hyperforin.
Wound-healing: The tincture promotes wound healing and tissue regeneration, helping chapped lips recover faster. The flavonoids and tannins in St. John's wort contribute to this medicinal property.
Moisturizing: St. John's wort tincture has moisturizing properties that help keep lips hydrated and prevent further chapping. This is due to the presence of essential oils and waxes in the plant.

Analgesic: The tincture provides pain relief for cracked and sore lips, making it easier to manage discomfort. Hypericin and flavonoids are the active constituents responsible for this analgesic effect.

Antimicrobial: St. John's wort tincture inhibits the growth of bacteria and fungi, reducing the risk of infection in chapped lips. The active constituents responsible for this antimicrobial property are hyperforin and hypericin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest St. John's Wort flowers and leaves, filling a clean jar about 3/4 full.
Submerge the plant material with enough 100-proof alcohol to cover it by at least an inch.
Allow the mixture to steep for 4-6 weeks in a cool, dark place, shaking it gently every few days.
Strain the mixture, discarding the plant material and retaining the liquid.
Use a cotton swab to apply the tincture to chapped lips as needed, avoiding contact with eyes.
remedy_parts: Flowers: St. John's wort flowers contain high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which can help soothe and heal chapped lips.
Leaves: The leaves of St. John's wort are rich in tannins and flavonoids, which have astringent and healing properties, making them useful for treating chapped lips.

Buds: The buds of St. John's wort contain high concentrations of active compounds, including hypericin and hyperforin, which have been shown to have wound-healing and anti-inflammatory effects, beneficial for chapped lips.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tincture helps with chapped lips because it has anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties that can soothe and heal dry, cracked lips. The tincture is made by extracting the active compounds from lavender flowers using alcohol or glycerin, which acts as a natural preservative. Lavender contains antioxidants that can protect the lips from environmental stressors and help repair damaged skin. The tincture can be applied directly to the lips or added to a lip balm for added benefits. Its calming scent can also help reduce stress and promote relaxation, which can indirectly contribute to overall lip health. Regular use of English lavender tincture can keep the lips soft, supple, and free from chapping.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: English lavender tincture helps reduce inflammation and irritation, providing relief for chapped lips caused by environmental factors or dryness. This property is due to the presence of linalyl acetate and linalool.
Wound healing: Lavender promotes tissue regeneration and speeds up the healing process of chapped lips, thanks to its high content of tannins, flavonoids, and essential oils.
Moisturizing: The tincture helps lock in moisture, keeping lips hydrated and preventing further chapping, due to the emollient properties of its lipids and waxes.
Analgesic: Lavender tincture has a soothing effect on painful, chapped lips, relieving discomfort and promoting faster recovery. This is attributed to the presence of linalool and linalyl acetate.

Antimicrobial: English lavender tincture inhibits the growth of bacteria and fungi, reducing the risk of infection in cracked lips. This property is due to the presence of volatile oils, flavonoids, and tannins.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh English lavender flowers, carefully removing wilted or discolored parts.
Chop flowers and place in a clean, dry glass jar, then pour 1 cup of vodka or grain alcohol (80-100 proof) over the flowers until fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking daily to ensure even extraction.
After the designated time, strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, squeezing out any excess liquid.
Transfer the strained tincture to a clean, dark glass bottle, storing in a cool, dark place for up to a year. Apply a small amount to chapped lips as needed for relief.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and moisturizing properties, lavender flowers help soothe and heal chapped lips.
Leaves: Containing anti-inflammatory compounds, lavender leaves help reduce redness and irritation in chapped lips.
Buds: High in essential oils, lavender buds provide a potent source for creating medicinal tinctures that help rejuvenate and protect chapped lips.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tincture helps with chapped lips because it has anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties that can soothe and hydrate dry, cracked lips. The tincture is made from the leaves of the lemon balm plant, which contains compounds that can help to reduce inflammation and irritation. Additionally, lemon balm has been shown to have antiviral properties, which may help to prevent the recurrence of chapped lips caused by viral infections. The tincture can be applied directly to the lips using a cotton swab or diluted in water and used as a mouth rinse. Its refreshing lemon scent and taste also make it a pleasant addition to lip care routine.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Lemon balm tincture can reduce inflammation and swelling, which is important for chapped lips as it helps alleviate irritation and promotes healing (active constituents
Moisturizing property: Lemon balm tincture has emollient properties that help lock in moisture, keeping lips hydrated and preventing further chapping (active constituents
Antiviral property: Lemon balm tincture can inhibit the herpes simplex virus, which can cause cold sores, a common cause of chapped lips (active constituent

Antioxidant property: Lemon balm tincture contains high levels of antioxidants that protect lips from environmental stressors, such as wind and sun, which can lead to chapped lips (active constituents
Analgesic property: Lemon balm tincture has a soothing effect that can help relieve pain and discomfort associated with chapped lips (active constituents
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of fresh lemon balm leaves, chop finely, and place in a clean jar.
Add 8 ounces of vodka or rum (80 proof) to the jar, ensuring all plant material is covered.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out excess liquid, and store tincture in a dark glass bottle.
Apply a small amount of tincture to chapped lips as needed, avoiding contact with eyes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain high levels of rosmarinic acid and tannins, which have anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that can help soothe and heal chapped lips.
Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and essential oils, they can help moisturize and protect chapped lips while providing a pleasant taste and aroma.
Stems: Although not as potent as the leaves or flowers, stems still contain beneficial compounds that can contribute to the overall effectiveness of the tincture in relieving chapped lips.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain tincture helps with chapped lips because it contains anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties that soothe and heal dry, cracked lips. The herb's mucilaginous quality creates a protective barrier on the lips, locking in moisture and shielding them from environmental stressors. Additionally, plantain's astringent properties help to tighten and repair damaged skin, promoting faster healing and preventing further chapping. Its rich supply of vitamins A, C, and E, as well as essential fatty acids, nourish and rejuvenate the lips, leaving them soft, supple, and moisturized.
remedy_properties: Wound healing: Plantain tincture contains allantoin, which promotes wound healing and skin regeneration, helping to soothe and repair chapped lips.
Anti-inflammatory: Plantain tincture has anti-inflammatory properties due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids, reducing inflammation and redness in chapped lips.
Moisturizing: Plantain tincture provides intensive hydration, as it contains mucilage that forms a protective barrier, sealing in moisture and alleviating dryness in chapped lips.
Antimicrobial: Plantain tecture exhibits antimicrobial activity, thanks to its tannins and saponins, which help prevent infection and speed up the healing process of chapped lips.
Soothing: Plantain tincture contains iridoid glycosides, which have a calming effect, reducing itching, pain, and discomfort associated with chapped lips.
remedy_recipe: Harvest plantain leaves from a clean, pesticide-free source, rinse, and dry thoroughly.
Chop or grind 1 cup of dried plantain leaves and place in a clean jar, then add 2 cups of 80-proof vodka.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, squeezing out the liquid.
Store the final tincture in a dark glass bottle and apply a small amount to chapped lips as needed.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in iridoids and flavonoids, plantain leaves are used in tinctures for their anti-inflammatory and wound-healing properties that help soothe chapped lips.
Seeds: High in mucilage, plantain seeds are used in tinctures to form a protective barrier on chapped lips, providing hydration and relief.
Flowers: Containing anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds, plantain flowers are used in tinctures to reduce redness, irritation, and promote healing of chapped lips.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Scotch pine tincture helps with chapped lips because it contains anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties that can soothe and heal dry, cracked lips. The herbal extracts found in scotch pine tincture, such as pinene and limonene, have been shown to have skin-healing benefits, including reducing redness, inflammation, and irritation. Additionally, the natural oils found in scotch pine can help lock in moisture, providing long-lasting hydration to chapped lips. By using scotch pine tincture regularly, you can help protect your lips from environmental stressors and keep them soft, smooth, and healthy.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Scotch pine tincture can reduce inflammation, which is important for chapped lips as it helps alleviate redness, swelling, and discomfort caused by dry, cracked skin. The active constituents responsible for its anti-inflammatory properties include flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of scotch pine tincture help prevent infection in chapped lips, protecting them from further irritation and potential complications. The primary active constituents responsible for its antimicrobial effects are alpha-pinene and limonene.

Analgesic: Scotch pine tincture has pain-relieving properties, which can help alleviate the discomfort associated with chapped lips. The active constituents responsible for its analgesic effects include bornyl acetate and camphene.

Emollient: Scotch pine tincture can help soften and soothe dry, cracked skin, promoting healing and preventing further chapping. The active constituents responsible for its emollient properties include monoterpenes and sesquiterpenes.

Circulation-boosting: Scotch pine tincture can improve blood flow to the lips, promoting healing and tissue regeneration. The active constituents responsible for its circulation-boosting properties include alpha-pinene and 1,8-cineole.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 cups of fresh scotch pine needles, rinse, and let dry for 2 hours.
Chop needles and combine with 1 cup of vodka and 1 cup of distilled water in a clean jar.
Seal jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain tincture through cheesecloth into a clean jar, discarding solids.
Apply tincture to chapped lips as needed, storing any remaining tincture in a cool, dark place for up to 6 months.
remedy_parts: Bark: The bark of the Scotch pine contains compounds that can help soothe and heal chapped lips due to its anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties.
Buds: The buds of the Scotch pine are rich in antioxidants and essential oils, making them an ideal ingredient for medicinal tinctures that can help protect and nourish chapped lips.
Leaves: The leaves of the Scotch pine contain tannins and volatile oils that have astringent and antiseptic properties, making them effective in treating and preventing chapped lips.
Seeds: The seeds of the Scotch pine contain fatty acids and vitamins that can help moisturize and regenerate the skin, making them a valuable addition to medicinal tinctures for chapped lips.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Narrowleaf plantain tincture helps with chapped lips because it contains anti-inflammatory and healing properties that can soothe and repair damaged skin. The herb is rich in mucilage, a substance that forms a protective layer over the skin, helping to lock in moisture and prevent further dryness. Additionally, it contains compounds such as allantoin and rosmarinic acid, which have been shown to promote tissue regeneration and reduce inflammation, making it an effective natural remedy for chapped and irritated lips. Its potent healing properties make it an excellent alternative to conventional lip balms and chapsticks, providing long-lasting relief and nourishment to dry and cracked lips.
remedy_properties: Wound healing: Narrowleaf plantain tincture promotes wound healing, which is important for chapped lips as it helps to repair damaged lip tissue and restore the skin's barrier function. The active constituents responsible for this property include allantoin, mucilage, and tannins.

Anti-inflammatory: Narrowleaf plantain tincture has anti-inflammatory properties, which is beneficial for chapped lips as it reduces redness, swelling, and discomfort caused by dry, cracked skin. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids, such as luteolin and apigenin.

Antimicrobial: Narrowleaf plantain tincture exhibits antimicrobial properties, which is crucial for chapped lips as it helps to prevent infection in broken or cracked skin. The active constituents responsible for this property include aucubin, catalpol, and tannins.

Moisturizing: Narrowleaf plantain tincture has moisturizing properties, which is essential for chapped lips as it helps to hydrate and soothe dry, flaky skin. The active constituents responsible for this property include mucilage and polysaccharides.

Analgesic: Narrowleaf plantain tincture has analgesic properties, which is important for chapped lips as it alleviates pain and discomfort associated with dry, cracked skin. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids, tannins, and essential oils.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 handfuls of fresh, mature narrowleaf plantain leaves (Plantago lanceolata) from a clean, pesticide-free environment.
Rinse the leaves thoroughly, then roughly chop and fill a clean, dry mason jar, leaving about an inch of space at the top.
Pour 80-proof vodka over the leaves until they are completely submerged, then secure the lid and store the jar in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks.
Shake the jar gently every few days to ensure even extraction.
After 4-6 weeks, strain the mixture through cheesecloth, squeezing out the liquid, and store the resulting tincture in a dark glass bottle. Apply a small amount of tincture to chapped lips as needed.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in mucilage, the leaves help soothe and protect chapped lips by forming a protective barrier.
Flowers: Containing anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, the flowers help reduce redness and irritation on chapped lips.
Seeds: High in essential fatty acids, the seeds provide intense moisturization and nourishment to heal and prevent chapped lips.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peach tincture helps with chapped lips because it contains antioxidants and vitamins that nourish and protect the delicate skin on the lips. The herbal extracts found in peach tincture have anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and discomfort. Additionally, the tincture can help to lock in moisture, preventing further dryness and promoting the natural healing process of the lips. The emollient properties of peach tincture make it an effective remedy for chapped lips, leaving them feeling soft, smooth, and hydrated.
remedy_properties: Moisturizing: Peach tincture contains emollient properties, which help to soothe and hydrate chapped lips, preventing further dryness and cracking. This is due to the presence of skin-replenishing fatty acids and antioxidants in peaches.

Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory properties of peach tincture help reduce redness, irritation, and swelling, providing relief for severely chapped lips. This is attributed to the presence of phenolic compounds and flavonoids in peaches.

Antioxidant: Peach tincture's high antioxidant content helps protect lips from environmental stressors and free radical damage, promoting faster healing of chapped lips. Active constituents include vitamins A and C, as well as various polyphenols.

Regenerative: Peach tincture encourages cellular regeneration and tissue repair, aiding in the recovery of damaged and chapped lips. This is due to the presence of vitamins A and C, which are essential for the maintenance and growth of collagen and other connective tissues.

Soothing: The soothing properties of peach tincture help alleviate pain and itching associated with chapped lips. This is attributed to the presence of tannins and other anti-irritant compounds in peaches.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ripe organic peaches into small pieces and place them in a clean glass jar.
Add 1 cup of dried organic chamomile flowers and 1/2 cup of dried organic calendula flowers to the jar.
Pour enough vodka or rum (80-100 proof) into the jar to completely cover the plant material, leaving about 1 inch of space at the top.
Seal the jar tightly and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve, squeezing out as much liquid as possible, and store the tincture in a dark glass bottle.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, peach flowers can help soothe and heal chapped lips.
Leaves: High in tannins, peach leaves can help to moisturize and protect chapped lips, reducing irritation and promoting healing.
Bark: Containing beneficial compounds, peach bark can provide relief for chapped lips by creating a protective barrier and reducing inflammation.
Seeds: Packed with essential fatty acids and vitamins, peach seeds can help to nourish and rejuvenate dry, chapped lips.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat chapped lips with herbal tinctures is by using a calendula tincture. This herb has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and heal dry, cracked lips. Dilute the tincture in equal parts with water and apply it directly to your lips using a cotton swab, up to three times a day. Another option is a sage tincture, which has antiseptic and astringent properties that can help dry lips recover faster. Remember to always dilute tinctures before using them and discontinue use if any irritation occurs.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to chapped lips that are treated with herbal tinctures are cold sores and angular cheilitis. These conditions can cause pain, redness, and inflammation around the mouth area. Herbal tinctures can provide relief and promote healing. For cold sores, tinctures made from lemon balm, St. John's wort, or licorice root can help reduce inflammation and combat the virus that causes them. Angular cheilitis, which is often caused by a fungal or bacterial infection, can be treated with tinctures containing calendula, myrrh, or goldenseal, which have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. However, it is essential to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new treatment.
